name: Is a handyman a good option if I don’t live near the property?
headline: Is a handyman a good option if I don’t live near the property?

mainEntity.name: Is a handyman a good option if I don’t live near the property?
mainEntity.text: Is a handyman a good option if I don’t live near the property?

acceptedAnswer.text: Handymen can be a very good option for people who are **not local or don’t have time**, as long as you set things up carefully. If you live elsewhere or travel a lot, you can use a handyman to: - Do seasonal maintenance like gutter cleaning, weatherstripping, and basic yard fixes - Check on minor issues renters report, like loose rails, sticky doors, or small leaks - Handle move‑in or move‑out punch lists between tenants To protect yourself: - Use written work orders describing what to do and what not to do - Ask for photos or short videos of the “before” and “after” for each job - Decide a dollar limit they can spend without checking with you (for example, “Call me if it will be over $300”) - Make sure you have a clear way to pay and get invoices, even when you’re not in town Pitfalls: - Giving someone free rein to do extra work you didn’t approve, which can lead to surprise bills - Not checking on recurring issues. If the same problem keeps coming back, you might need a higher‑level inspection by a licensed contractor, roofer, plumber, or electrician. With clear communication and boundaries, a good handyman can be your “eyes and hands” when you can’t be at the property yourself.
